steps.title: Use vertex form
steps.content:
A quadratic with vertex $(h,k)$ has form:
$$
f(t)=a(t-h)^2+k
$$
The maximum occurs at $t=1.9$ and height $57.76$, so the model must contain:
$$
(t-1.9)^2+57.76
$$

steps.title: Choose the opening direction
steps.content: The object reaches a maximum height, so the parabola opens downward. Therefore the coefficient of the squared term must be negative.

steps.title: Match the model
steps.content: The only choice with vertex $(1.9,57.76)$ and a negative leading coefficient is $f(t)=-16(t-1.9)^2+57.76$. The answer is A.
